market_summary: Regime: mixed-to-risk-on. SPY/QQQ/IWM remain above MA50/MA200, with IWM 1m +4.30% and QQQ 3m +21.82%, but the last month shows a pullback under the surface in mega-cap growth.

Leadership remains concentrated in semicap/cyber: KLAC 1m +27.35%, 3m +53.74%; TSM 1m +9.23%, 3m +28.73%; PANW 1m +9.47%, 3m +81.45%. Gold and crypto trend are weak, so no defensive metals or crypto sleeve is justified.

Go/no-go: not a rebalance day and edge does not clear turnover hurdle. Qualitative refresh did not produce verified thesis damage severe enough to override the weekly cadence, so portfolio stays unchanged.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows soft near-term tape: 1m -12.03%, below MA50, and momentum composite only +4.11; qualitative follow-up on fundamentals/news is UNKNOWN.
Pack shows weakest momentum among current holdings: 3m -1.16%, 1m -6.53%, below MA50; no new qualitative evidence today to justify turnover.
Short-term trend weakened: 1m -9.79% and below MA50 despite still strong 12m +109.62%; qualitative key-ratio/news refresh partly UNKNOWN.

thesis: Catalyst: none strong enough to justify an off-schedule change one day after the last rebalance.
Factor edge: preserving exposure to the current semicap/cyber leadership cluster avoids paying turnover into a still risk-on but choppy tape.
Variant view: recent weakness in AMZN/GOOGL/COST may prove transient while the strongest holdings KLAC/TSM/PANW continue to carry portfolio alpha over the next 30 days.
risks: Macro: a growth or rates shock could hit the portfolio's large tech concentration despite no action today.
Idiosyncratic: if fresh negative guidance/news emerges in weaker holdings, delayed action could cost relative performance.
Execution/liquidity: no immediate execution risk from holding steady, but waiting can reduce flexibility if dispersion widens further.
falsifiable_checks: Within 30 days, if KLAC, TSM, and PANW collectively lose MA50 support while SPY stays above MA50, the current leadership thesis is failing.
Within 30 days, if AMZN or COST underperform SPY by 10 percentage points or more on the rolling 1m window, thesis-damage pressure for the next scheduled rebalance rises materially.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: the regime is still constructive, but not enough has changed in 24 hours to justify another trade after yesterday's replacement. Bull: semicap/cyber leaders continue compounding and the portfolio benefits from low turnover. Bear: the recent mega-cap pullback deepens and current laggards worsen. Even in the bear case, today's expected edge from trading does not clearly exceed trading-cost and timing drag in a paper weekly process.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights—KLAC/TSM/PANW remain top score leaders on 30d/90d momentum and trend; AMZN/COST weakest among holdings; NVDA/GOOGL/AVGO short-term trend softer but still above MA200. Fundamentals/news refresh—NVDA quality remains exceptional (op margin 65.6%, ROE 104.4%, ROIC 119.7%, rev growth 15.4%, EPS growth 21.0%, P/E 38.0, PEG 1.81); GOOGL revenue/net income snapshot strong but key ratios/estimates UNKNOWN; PANW and AMZN qualitative fundamentals UNKNOWN. Risk budget—IT 66%, Comm 14%, Cons Disc 12%, Staples 8%; no crypto; single-name max 14%. Scenario stress—equity index shock: portfolio likely underperforms SPY due to tech beta; rates shock: mixed, semis/software most exposed; commodity shock: limited direct risk; USD shock: some ADR sensitivity via TSM. Execution plan—hold unchanged until next scheduled rebalance unless severe thesis damage emerges. Monitoring/kill-switch—watch AMZN/COST relative weakness, GOOGL MA200 hold, and whether KLAC/TSM/PANW lose MA50 leadership within the next 30 days.
